title: Hours_diff
excerpt:
When I use HOURS_DIFF function it return a wrong value 
HOURS_DIFF for 9h00,11h30 return 2 instead of 2.5

posts.author: pierreotis (Pierre Otis)
posts.cookedHtml:
When I use HOURS_DIFF function it return a wrong value
HOURS_DIFF for 9h00,11h30 return 2 instead of 2.5

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
pierreotis (Pierre Otis)
posts.cookedHtml: What I need is to calculate duration. When I try to convert 1h30 to a number either 1.5 h or 90 min it returns a wrong number. The main purpose is to be able to graph the duration.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re not explaining which types of fields are involved, not publishing the syntax that doesn’t work for you, not giving a concrete example with values involved… Difficult to help you out…

posts.author: RyanHanna (Ryan Hanna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello
I am having the same problem as Pierre.

When I enter:

SUM(HOURS_DIFF({END},{START}))

It returns 8 hours between 9:00am and 5:30pm

It will report 08:30 if I enter only:

HOURS_DIFF({END},{START}

But that does not work because we need to calculate full hours in payroll and the XX:YY format does not work.

What do I need to do to get this to report 8.5 hours as we require, please?
